title: StoreHeadAnchor
description: An external, out-of-mesh attestation of a host's evidence-store head (chp-v0.2.md section 12 External anchoring, proposal 0013). An external did:key SSHSIG-countersigns chp-stable-v1({kind, host_id, sequence, store_head, anchored_at}) under SSHSIG namespace chp-store-head-anchor. Independent of the witnessing peer set: an anchored head survives even if every witness colludes. This is the signed-checkpoint form; real Rekor/Sigstore Merkle-inclusion proofs are a named deferral.

sequence.description: The host's global store sequence at anchoring time.

store_head.description: Store-head root at `sequence`: chp-store-head-v1 flat digest, or the chp-store-head-v2 RFC 6962 Merkle root (proposal 0019). Countersigned opaquely, so the scheme does not change the anchor.

store_head_scheme.description: Which store-head scheme produced `store_head` (proposal 0019). Absent → chp-store-head-v1. Advisory (self-validated: recomputing the root under it must equal the countersigned `store_head`), so it is NOT in the countersigned bytes; it lets a stranger recompute + verify an inclusion proof.

anchored_at.description: ISO-8601 anchoring time (part of the countersigned bytes).

anchor.description: The external anchor. Mirrors the §3.1 identity DID anchor, applied to a store head.

type.description: Anchor type. `did` = an external did:key SSHSIG countersignature. (Future: `rekor` transparency-log inclusion — deferred.)

did.description: The external anchor authority's did:key (ed25519). A notary or a transparency-log checkpoint key.

countersignature.description: Armored SSHSIG over chp-stable-v1({kind, host_id, sequence, store_head, anchored_at}), namespace chp-store-head-anchor.
